reasoning: The market price for this question on Manifold was 45% as of early April 2026. Recent news confirms significant intelligence-sharing rifts between the US and its allies. In late 2025, the UK paused intelligence sharing regarding maritime operations due to US strikes in the Caribbean, and the Netherlands explicitly restricted intelligence sharing over human rights and political concerns. In January 2026, NATO officials restricted intelligence sharing to the US following disputes over Greenland. While these actions represent partial restrictions or "scaling back" rather than a total, permanent cessation of all intelligence cooperation from an EU/NATO country, the situation remains highly volatile. The escalating tensions make a complete halt by at least one member state by the end of 2026 a genuine possibility. Given the recent trajectory of events (e.g., the January 2026 NATO/Greenland developments), the market's 45% probability seems well-calibrated to the risk of a full intelligence stoppage. I am anchoring to this 45% estimate for the end-of-2026 horizon. (Note: Outputting 3 identical probabilities to match the 'N, /, A' parsed resolution dates which represent a single N/A timeframe).
